proposed_regression_tests: A. block-enabled ne_storage with zero standard ratings but positive block ratings can charge/discharge/store energy after investment
B. block-enabled generator with zero/small standard pmax but positive p_block_max is not limited by standard pmax
C. non-block storage still obeys standard energy/charge/discharge ratings
D. storage energy balance remains unchanged
E. block investment cost counted exactly once
F. standard ne_storage investment cost not double-counted with block investment cost
H. gSCR reconstruction residual remains near zero for positive g_min
open_modeling_decisions: Should z_strg_ne remain or be replaced by pure n_block logic for block-enabled candidate storage?
If z_strg_ne remains, what explicit coupling to n_block is intended?
Should block-enabled storage ignore standard energy/charge/discharge ratings entirely?
Should block-enabled generators ignore standard pmax/qmax or keep conservative intersection?
Which final storage policy is default in 24h CAPEXP studies?
